title: Init creates standard project structure
description: Validates that testforge init creates a new project directory with all required subdirectories and config file

steps.action: Verify subdirectories exist: .testforge/, inputs/, output/, evidence/, scripts/, cases/, analysis/
Verify .testforge/config.yaml exists and is valid YAML
steps.expected_result: config.yaml exists and parses without error

expected_result: A fully scaffolded project directory with standard structure is created

title: Init fails when directory already exists
description: Validates that testforge init does not overwrite an existing project directory
preconditions: Directory myproject/ already exists with content

steps.action: Create directory myproject/ with a dummy file inside

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 1 and displays error message about existing directory

steps.action: Verify original content in myproject/ is unchanged
steps.expected_result: Dummy file still present and unmodified

expected_result: Init refuses to overwrite existing directory and exits with error

title: Init displays help with --help flag
description: Validates R-002: init subcommand shows help text and exits 0

steps.expected_result: Help text is displayed describing usage, arguments, and options

expected_result: Help text displayed and process exits with code 0

title: Analyze parses a PDF document and extracts features
description: Validates that testforge analyze can ingest a PDF and produce structured analysis output with features, personas, and business rules

steps.action: Verify analysis/ directory contains output files
Inspect analysis output for features, personas, and business_rules sections
steps.expected_result: All three sections are present and contain extracted data

expected_result: PDF is parsed and analysis output contains features, personas, and business rules

title: Analyze handles unsupported file format gracefully
description: Validates that analyze rejects unsupported file types with a clear error

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 1 or warns about unsupported format

steps.action: Check stderr/stdout for meaningful error message
steps.expected_result: Error message indicates unsupported file format

expected_result: Unsupported format is rejected with informative error, no crash

title: Analyze handles empty inputs directory
description: Validates behavior when no input documents are present

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 1 and displays error about missing inputs

expected_result: Clear error message about no input documents found

title: Analyze parses multiple document types in single run
description: Validates that analyze processes a mix of PDF, DOCX, and image files together

steps.action: Place spec.pdf, requirements.docx, and wireframe.png in inputs/
steps.expected_result: All three files exist in inputs/

steps.action: Verify analysis output references content from all three documents
steps.expected_result: Extracted features/personas reference data from each input source

expected_result: All supported document types are processed and merged into unified analysis

title: Generate creates test cases from analysis results
description: Validates that testforge generate produces functional test cases from prior analysis output
preconditions: testforge analyze has been run successfully

steps.action: Verify cases/ directory contains generated test case files
steps.expected_result: One or more test case files exist in cases/

steps.action: Inspect test case files for required fields (id, title, steps, expected_result)
steps.expected_result: Each test case contains all required fields

steps.action: Verify test cases reference features identified during analysis
steps.expected_result: Test cases are traceable to analyzed features

expected_result: Functional test cases are generated with proper structure and traceability

title: Generate fails without prior analysis
description: Validates that generate requires analysis output to exist before running
preconditions: analysis/ directory is empty or missing

steps.expected_result: Error indicates analysis must be run first

expected_result: Generate refuses to run without analysis data and provides actionable error

title: Generate produces manual checklist output
description: Validates that generate creates manual QA checklists alongside automated test cases

steps.action: Check output for manual checklist files
steps.expected_result: Manual checklist file exists with human-readable steps

steps.action: Verify checklist items have pass/fail checkboxes or status fields
steps.expected_result: Each checklist item is structured for manual verification

expected_result: Manual checklists are generated alongside automated test cases

title: Script generates Playwright test from test cases
description: Validates that testforge script produces valid Playwright browser test scripts

steps.action: Verify scripts/ directory contains generated .py or .ts files
Inspect generated scripts for valid Playwright syntax (imports, page interactions, assertions)
steps.expected_result: Scripts contain valid Playwright API calls

steps.action: Verify scripts reference test case IDs in comments or test names
steps.expected_result: Scripts are traceable to source test cases

expected_result: Syntactically valid Playwright scripts are generated from test cases

title: Script generation fails without test cases
description: Validates that script command requires existing test cases

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 1 with error about missing test cases

expected_result: Script generation fails gracefully when no test cases exist

title: Run executes test scripts and collects evidence
description: Validates that testforge run executes generated scripts and stores evidence artifacts

steps.action: Verify evidence/ directory contains execution artifacts (screenshots, logs, results)
Verify execution results include pass/fail status per test
steps.expected_result: Each executed test has a recorded status

expected_result: Test scripts execute successfully with evidence and results collected

title: Run reports failure when test script errors out
description: Validates that run properly captures and reports script execution failures
preconditions: scripts/ contains a script that will fail (e.g., targeting unreachable URL)

steps.action: Place a test script targeting http://localhost:99999 (unreachable) in scripts/

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 1 or reports failures

steps.action: Verify execution results show failed status with error details
steps.expected_result: Failure reason is captured in results

expected_result: Failed test execution is captured with error details, not silently ignored

title: Run with no scripts directory returns error
description: Validates that run handles missing scripts gracefully

steps.action: Verify error message indicates no scripts to execute

expected_result: Run fails with informative message when no scripts exist

title: Report generates markdown report from results
description: Validates that testforge report produces a markdown test report from execution results
preconditions: testforge run has been executed with results available

steps.action: Verify output/ contains a .md report file
Inspect report for summary statistics (total, passed, failed)
Verify report includes individual test results with status
steps.expected_result: Each test case result is listed with pass/fail

expected_result: Well-structured markdown report is generated with execution summary and details

title: Report generates HTML format when specified
description: Validates HTML report output option

steps.action: Verify output/ contains a .html report file
Open HTML file and verify it renders valid HTML with test results
steps.expected_result: HTML is well-formed and displays results

expected_result: HTML report is generated with proper formatting

title: Report fails without execution results
description: Validates that report requires prior test execution data

steps.action: Ensure no execution results exist in project

steps.expected_result: Error indicates no execution results to report on

expected_result: Report generation fails with clear error when no results exist

title: Manual workflow start initializes checklist session
description: Validates that testforge manual start begins a new manual QA session with checklist

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 0 and displays session ID

steps.action: Verify a manual session is created with all checklist items in pending state
steps.expected_result: Session exists with items ready for verification
Shows 0% completion with all items pending

expected_result: Manual QA session is initialized with full checklist

title: Manual workflow check marks item and finish completes session
description: Validates the check and finish subcommands of manual workflow
preconditions: Active manual QA session exists with pending items

steps.expected_result: Item marked as passed, exit code 0

steps.action: Mark all remaining items as pass/fail

steps.expected_result: Session completed, results saved, exit code 0

expected_result: Manual items can be checked and session can be finalized

title: Manual finish without completing all items warns user
description: Validates behavior when finishing a session with unchecked items
preconditions: Active manual QA session with some items still pending

steps.action: Mark only 1 of N items, leaving others pending

steps.expected_result: Session finishes with warning about incomplete items or exit code 1

steps.action: Verify results indicate which items were not checked
steps.expected_result: Unchecked items are flagged in output

expected_result: Finishing with unchecked items produces a warning or is handled gracefully

title: TUI launches interactive terminal interface
description: Validates that testforge tui starts the Textual-based terminal UI

steps.expected_result: TUI application launches and displays dashboard screen

steps.action: Verify dashboard, cases, runner, and manual screens are accessible via navigation
Press quit key binding to exit

expected_result: TUI launches with all expected screens and exits cleanly

title: TUI handles missing project gracefully
description: Validates TUI behavior when launched outside a testforge project
preconditions: Current directory is NOT a testforge project

steps.expected_result: Error message displayed or TUI shows empty state with guidance

expected_result: TUI either refuses to launch with error or handles missing project gracefully

title: Selftest runs built-in test scripts successfully
description: Validates that testforge selftest discovers and executes built-in shell tests

steps.action: Verify output lists each test script with pass/fail status

expected_result: All selftest scripts execute and results are reported

title: Selftest reports failure when a test script fails
description: Validates that selftest properly surfaces individual test failures
preconditions: At least one selftest script is designed to fail or is modified to fail

steps.action: Modify a selftest script to exit with code 1
Verify output identifies the failing test by name
steps.expected_result: Failed test name and error are visible in output

expected_result: Selftest exits with failure code and identifies which tests failed

title: Projects lists all testforge projects in current directory
description: Validates that testforge projects discovers and lists project directories
preconditions: Current directory contains two or more testforge project subdirectories

steps.action: Create two project directories with testforge init proj1 -y && testforge init proj2 -y
Verify output lists both proj1 and proj2
steps.expected_result: Both project names appear in output

expected_result: All testforge projects in the directory are listed

title: Projects returns empty list when no projects exist
description: Validates behavior when no testforge projects are found
preconditions: Current directory contains no testforge projects

steps.expected_result: Command exits with code 0 and outputs empty list or informational message

expected_result: Empty result is returned gracefully, not an error

title: CLI returns exit code 0 on successful command
description: Validates R-001: successful commands return exit code 0
expected_result: Exit code 0 is returned for successful execution

title: CLI returns exit code 1 on failed command
description: Validates R-001: failed commands return exit code 1
expected_result: Exit code 1 is returned for failed execution

title: Non-interactive mode skips all prompts with -y flag
description: Validates R-003: the -y flag suppresses all interactive confirmations

steps.action: Run testforge init autoproj -y in a non-interactive shell (no TTY)
steps.expected_result: Command completes without hanging for user input

steps.action: Verify project was created without any prompts
steps.expected_result: Project directory exists with standard structure

expected_result: All commands complete without interactive prompts when -y is passed

title: Full Pipeline: Web App Requirements to HTML Report
description: QA Engineer initializes a project, analyzes a product requirements PDF, generates test cases and Playwright scripts, executes them against a staging environment, and produces an HTML report for stakeholders.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
A product requirements PDF document is available locally
A staging web application URL is accessible for testing
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge init webapp-qa' to create a new project with standard directory structure
Copy the product requirements PDF into the inputs/ directory
Run 'testforge analyze' to parse the PDF and extract features, personas, and business rules
Review the analysis output in the analysis/ directory to confirm extracted items match expectations
Run 'testforge generate' to create functional test cases and use-case scenarios from the analysis
Review generated test cases in the cases/ directory for completeness and accuracy
Run 'testforge script' to generate Playwright browser test scripts from the test cases
Verify that scripts appear in the scripts/ directory with proper Playwright syntax
Run 'testforge run' to execute the generated scripts against the staging environment
Confirm that evidence (screenshots, logs) is collected in the evidence/ directory
Run 'testforge report --format html' to generate an HTML test report from execution results
Open the generated HTML report and verify it contains pass/fail summaries and evidence links
expected_outcome: A complete HTML test report is generated in output/ containing all test results, pass/fail status, and linked evidence artifacts. The report accurately reflects the execution of tests derived from the original requirements document.

title: Manual QA Workflow with TUI for Mobile App Testing
description: QA Engineer initializes a project, analyzes a PPTX design spec, generates a manual checklist, then uses the TUI and manual workflow commands to track progress through manual testing of a mobile application.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
A PPTX design specification for a mobile app is available
The mobile application under test is accessible on a test device or emulator
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge init mobile-manual-qa' to create a new project
Copy the PPTX design specification into the inputs/ directory
Run 'testforge analyze' to extract features, personas, and UI flows from the PPTX
Run 'testforge generate' to produce manual checklist items from the analysis
Run 'testforge manual start' to begin a structured manual testing session
Launch 'testforge tui' to open the interactive terminal UI
Navigate to the manual screen in the TUI to view the checklist
Perform manual testing steps on the mobile app, marking items as pass/fail in the TUI
Run 'testforge manual progress' to check how many items remain
Run 'testforge manual check' to verify status of specific checklist items
Complete all remaining manual test items through the TUI interface
Run 'testforge manual finish' to close the manual testing session
Run 'testforge report --format markdown' to generate a markdown report of manual test results
expected_outcome: All manual checklist items are tracked to completion through the TUI and manual workflow. A markdown report is generated summarizing manual test results with pass/fail statuses for each checklist item derived from the design specification.

title: Multi-Document Analysis with Mixed Input Types
description: QA Engineer analyzes multiple input documents of different formats (PDF, DOCX, images, and a URL) in a single project to produce a consolidated set of test cases covering all sources.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
Multiple input documents are available: a PDF spec, a DOCX user guide, wireframe images, and a live URL to an existing web app
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge init multi-source-project' to create a new project
Copy the PDF specification, DOCX user guide, and wireframe images into the inputs/ directory
Run 'testforge analyze' providing the local files and the URL as inputs
Verify that the analysis/ directory contains extracted features from all document types
Confirm that personas and business rules are consolidated across all input sources
Run 'testforge generate' to create test cases covering features from all analyzed documents
Review generated cases in cases/ to ensure traceability back to each input source
Run 'testforge script' to generate Playwright scripts for the web-applicable test cases
Run 'testforge run' to execute the browser tests against the live URL
Run 'testforge report' to generate a consolidated test report
expected_outcome: Analysis successfully processes all input formats (PDF, DOCX, images, URL). Generated test cases cover features extracted from every source. The final report reflects execution results for the consolidated test suite.

title: Multi-Project Management Across Concurrent Testing Efforts
description: QA Engineer manages multiple TestForge projects simultaneously, using project listing to navigate between them, running the self-test suite to verify the installation, and executing tests across projects.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
A working directory exists where multiple projects will be created
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge selftest' to verify the installation is functioning correctly before starting work
Confirm all self-test scripts in tests/selftest/ pass successfully
Run 'testforge init project-alpha' to create the first project
Run 'testforge init project-beta' to create the second project
Run 'testforge projects' from the parent directory to list all TestForge projects
Verify that both project-alpha and project-beta appear in the project listing
Add input documents to project-alpha and run 'testforge analyze' followed by 'testforge generate'
Add different input documents to project-beta and run 'testforge analyze' followed by 'testforge generate'
Run 'testforge run' in project-alpha to execute its test suite
Run 'testforge run' in project-beta to execute its test suite
Run 'testforge projects' again to see updated status of both projects
Generate reports for both projects using 'testforge report'
expected_outcome: Self-test suite passes confirming a valid installation. Both projects are independently managed with separate analysis, test cases, and execution results. The projects command correctly discovers and lists all TestForge projects with their current status.

title: Developer Extends Pipeline with Custom Connectors and Runs Full Cycle
description: Developer sets up a TestForge project, analyzes an API specification, generates test cases, creates custom HTTP and shell connectors for non-browser testing, executes tests through those connectors, and validates the complete pipeline.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
An OpenAPI/Swagger specification document is available for a REST API
The target REST API is running and accessible
Developer has familiarity with TestForge's connector and assertion extension points
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge init api-testing-project' to create a new project
Copy the API specification document into the inputs/ directory
Run 'testforge analyze' to extract API endpoints, request/response schemas, and business rules
Run 'testforge generate' to create test cases for the API endpoints
Review the generated test cases and identify those requiring HTTP and shell connectors rather than browser tests
Implement a custom HTTP connector that sends REST requests and validates response codes and payloads
Implement a custom shell connector that runs curl-based smoke tests and database seed scripts
Implement custom assertions for API-specific validation (JSON schema validation, response time thresholds)
Configure the project's .testforge/config.yaml to register the custom connectors and assertions
Run 'testforge run' to execute tests using the custom connectors
Verify that evidence is collected for both HTTP and shell connector executions
Run 'testforge report --format html' to generate the final report
Inspect the report to confirm custom connector results are properly represented
expected_outcome: Custom HTTP and shell connectors execute successfully alongside standard connectors. Custom assertions validate API-specific behavior. The final HTML report includes results from all connector types with proper evidence collection and pass/fail attribution.

title: Developer Integrates TestForge into CI/CD with Self-Test Validation
description: Developer configures TestForge for non-interactive CI/CD pipeline execution, runs self-tests to validate the environment, executes the full analysis-generate-run-report pipeline in headless mode, and verifies all outputs are produced.
preconditions: TestForge is installed in a CI/CD environment (e.g., GitHub Actions runner)
An LLM API key is configured as a CI environment variable
A pre-existing TestForge project with input documents is checked into the repository
CI/CD pipeline script has access to shell execution
scenario_steps: Run 'testforge selftest' as a CI pre-check to verify the installation is healthy in the CI environment
Assert that all self-test scripts pass before proceeding with the pipeline
Run 'testforge init ci-test-run' or navigate to the pre-existing project directory
Run 'testforge analyze' in non-interactive mode to process the input documents
Verify that analysis output files are created in the analysis/ directory
Run 'testforge generate' in non-interactive mode to produce test cases
Verify that test case files are created in the cases/ directory
Run 'testforge script' to generate Playwright scripts for browser-based tests
Run 'testforge run' in headless mode to execute all generated test scripts
Verify that evidence artifacts (screenshots, logs) are captured in evidence/
Run 'testforge report --format markdown' to generate a CI-friendly markdown report
Parse the report output to extract pass/fail counts for CI status reporting
Fail the CI pipeline if any critical tests failed based on the report
expected_outcome: The full TestForge pipeline executes successfully in a non-interactive CI/CD environment. Self-tests validate the environment. All pipeline stages produce expected outputs. The markdown report provides machine-parseable results suitable for CI status gates.

title: TUI-Driven Dashboard Monitoring During Test Execution
description: QA Engineer uses the TUI interface as the primary interaction method to monitor test execution in real-time, review test cases, trigger runs, and track manual QA progress from the dashboard.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
A TestForge project already exists with completed analysis and generated test cases and scripts
scenario_steps: Launch 'testforge tui' to open the interactive terminal UI
Navigate to the dashboard screen and verify it shows the project summary and status
Switch to the cases screen to browse all generated test cases
Review individual test case details including steps, expected results, and priority
Switch to the runner screen and initiate a test execution run from within the TUI
Monitor the execution progress on the runner screen as tests execute in real-time
Observe test pass/fail indicators updating as each test completes
Switch to the manual screen to view any manual checklist items
Start a manual QA session from the TUI and mark several items as completed
Return to the dashboard screen to see the updated overall project status
Verify the dashboard reflects both automated execution results and manual progress
expected_outcome: The TUI provides a fully functional interface for monitoring and managing all aspects of the testing workflow. Dashboard accurately reflects project state. Test execution can be triggered and monitored in real-time. Manual QA progress is tracked seamlessly within the same interface.

title: Iterative Test Refinement: Analyze, Generate, Execute, Fix, Re-run
description: QA Engineer goes through multiple iterations of the test pipeline, re-analyzing updated documents, regenerating test cases after requirements change, and re-executing to verify fixes.
preconditions: TestForge is installed and accessible via CLI
An LLM API key is configured in the environment
An existing TestForge project with a completed first-pass analysis, generated cases, and initial execution results exists
An updated version of the requirements document is available reflecting recent changes
scenario_steps: Navigate to the existing project directory
Run 'testforge run' to execute the existing test suite and confirm current baseline results
Run 'testforge report' to generate a baseline report showing initial pass/fail status
Replace the input document in inputs/ with the updated version reflecting new requirements
Run 'testforge analyze' again to re-process the updated document
Compare new analysis output with the previous analysis to identify changed features and rules
Run 'testforge generate' to regenerate test cases incorporating the updated requirements
Run 'testforge script' to regenerate Playwright scripts for new and modified test cases
Run 'testforge run' to execute the updated test suite
Review evidence/ for any new failures caused by requirements changes
Run 'testforge report' to generate an updated report
Compare the new report with the baseline to identify regression or new coverage gaps
expected_outcome: The iterative workflow produces updated test artifacts at each stage. Re-analysis captures changed requirements. Regenerated test cases reflect the updates. The comparison between baseline and updated reports clearly shows the impact of requirements changes on test results.

title: Manual check: Project Init
description: Verify Project Init manually: testforge init creates a new project directory with standard structure (.testforge/config.yaml, inputs/, output/, evidence/, scripts/, cases/, analysis/)
check_steps: Navigate to the Project Init area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly
Perform the primary action and observe results
Check error handling with invalid input

check_steps: Navigate to the Document Analysis area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly
Perform the primary action and observe results
Check error handling with invalid input

title: Manual check: Test Case Generation

check_steps: Navigate to the Test Case Generation area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly
Perform the primary action and observe results

pass_criteria: Test Case Generation behaves as documented

check_steps: Navigate to the Script Generation area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly

check_steps: Navigate to the Test Execution area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly

title: Manual check: Manual QA Workflow

check_steps: Navigate to the Manual QA Workflow area

Perform the primary action and observe results
Check error handling with invalid input
pass_criteria: Manual QA Workflow behaves as documented

check_steps: Navigate to the Self-Test Suite area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly
Perform the primary action and observe results
Check error handling with invalid input

title: Manual check: Multi-Project Management
description: Verify Multi-Project Management manually: testforge projects lists all TestForge projects found in the current directory
check_steps: Navigate to the Multi-Project Management area
Verify all UI elements are displayed correctly
Perform the primary action and observe results
Check error handling with invalid input
